title: mfa — retention windows
description: MFA retention is compliance-driven, not product-driven. Disabled WebAuthn credentials retain for 90 days (audit-trail hold). MFA challenges are ephemeral (5-minute TTL, hard-purged on expiry). Successful challenges leave activity_log entries for compliance correlation. Failed challenges retain for 90 days to support suspicious-activity forensics. Enrollment previews are strictly ephemeral (5-minute Redis TTL, no durable trail).
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(e) (storage limitation) — no MFA material outlives the audit window
gdpr Art. 17 (right to erasure) — WebauthnCredential.identity_id CASCADE guarantees credential erasure follows identity erasure
nist 800-63B § 4.4.1 (authenticator lifecycle) — disabled authenticators retained for audit but not usable
soc2 CC7.2 (system monitoring) — failed-challenge retention feeds the anomaly-detection window
iso 27001 A.5.16 (identity management) — full lifecycle audit-log preserved

hot.days: while the referring Identity is active

purge.trigger: user deletes via DELETE /me/mfa/webauthn/credentials/{id} OR identity/identity's cascade on Identity hard-delete
purge.notes: Deleting an active credential fires WebauthnCredentialDeleted(trigger=user_action|identity_erasure_cascade). If this is the caller's last MFA factor, MfaDisabled fires as a follow-on.
Fires WebauthnCredentialDeleted(trigger=retention_purge). The 90-day window supports the auditor's 'was credential X active on date T' question.

retention.entity: MFA challenge (auth_mfa_challenges row — owned by identity/auth)

purge.trigger: expires_at < now() OR consumed on successful verify OR invalidated on max_attempts
purge.notes: Ephemeral by design. No audit trail on the challenge row itself — the MfaChallengeSucceeded / MfaChallengeFailed events carry the audit signal into the activity_log.

purge.trigger: TTL elapsed OR round-2 confirm consumed the envelope OR PurgeUnconfirmedEnrollmentsJob evicted
purge.notes: Ephemeral by design. The plaintext TOTP secret exists ONLY in this envelope during the 5-minute confirmation window; expiry destroys it.

purge.trigger: TTL elapsed OR round-2 register consumed the challenge OR PurgeUnconfirmedEnrollmentsJob evicted
activity_log's per-tenant retention policy (identity/user + observability modules own the retention)
purge.action: archive → cold-store → hard delete per tenant tier
purge.notes: The MFA challenge outcome is part of the tenant's audit trail. This module does not own the retention curve — activity_log does — but declaring the reference here documents the flow.

hot.days: 90 (hot) — longer than success for forensic reasons

purge.notes: Failed challenges are retained longer because they feed the MfaSuspiciousActivityDetector's rolling window. The detector reads the last 15 minutes; retention beyond that is for post-incident forensics.

retention.entity: MFA recovery codes (JSONB on Identity — owned by identity/identity)

purge.trigger: user regenerates OR consumes individually OR disables MFA OR Identity is hard-deleted
purge.action: overwrite JSONB with new hashes / remove individual entry / set to NULL / delete via cascade
purge.notes: Individual codes are single-use: once consumed, they're removed from the JSONB array in the same transaction. The whole array is regenerated as one atomic replace.

hold_period_extensions.description: The disabled-credential retention window (default 90 days) can be extended per Identity by compliance officers when a specific credential is under legal investigation. Extension is stored as a per-credential metadata flag (`credential_retention_hold_until`) that overrides the default. CleanupDisabledWebauthnCredentialsJob honours the per-credential override.
hold_period_extensions.how_to_extend: Via the `mfa:describe-method` CLI with `--set-hold-until=<date>` (planned CLI extension — not shipped in Day 1 blueprint).
hold_period_extensions.audit_trail: Every extension is written to the compliance-audit log with the compliance officer as actor + free-form justification.
